item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's an interesting utilization of the anomalous property of an SCP object. Realy good story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Small running wheels are to be provided in exchange for good behavior and to encourage exercise.

Maybe it's just me, but seeing the article start off with the Foundation treating a group of sapient humanoids like pet hamsters is… a little unsettling, and not in a way that benefits the article.

The logs give me the impression of a -J article. Silly dialogue can work in normal skips, but the actions aren't very believable to me. The MTF treating the gnomes like they're trying to catch a cat, a D-class seemingly escorting an anomaly alone (why switch to D-class midway?) and being able to go and take some sugar back into containment, seems too wacky and makes the Foundation look really incompetent.

And this is a lesser concern, but even though much of the content is focused on recovery logs, it doesn't mention why/how the Foundation got the info in the first place and deemed them a low risk despite not being aware of their nature.

I kind of enjoyed the gnomes pitting each other but other than that I feel like the article doesn't make use of the fact they're garden gnomes, they could have easily been something like geese and I don't feel like it would have changed.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: This is like the fourth time I’ve been gnomed since I started writing this. I can only blame myself.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
This is my entry for Jamcon day 1! I do not like gnomes and wanted to write an article where they blow up. I hope y'all enjoy.

Thanks to D-Fish, DrFullham, and ImmortalPhoenix43 for crit.
